page: Hetzner Cloud cax31 is an ARM64-based virtual server powered by the Ampere Altra processor family using Neoverse-N1 cores. It features 8 vCPUs based on 8 physical cores with 1 thread per core under a shared CPU allocation model, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 1.95 GB of memory per core, and is bundled with 160 GB of local SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for multi-core operations, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 14455.92 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5731.0. Memory read bandwidth is measured at 560.18 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 79.75 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1063.0 and a weak Passmark CPU score of 6189.29. Cryptographic and decompression tasks also show lower relative performance, with a weak Passmark encryption test score of 1305.95 and a poor LLM text generation speed of 31.49 tokens/sec on small models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cax31 utilizes a shared ARM64 architecture to deliver a high physical core count and local SSD storage at a budget-friendly resource density. This makes it highly suitable for workloads that benefit from multi-threaded execution but do not require sustained, dedicated single-core performance. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 8.6 GB/sec, Redis database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose utility tasks.
description: Hetzner Cloud cax31 is an ARM64 virtual server configured with 8 vCPUs on an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.0 GHz with shared CPU allocation. It features 16.0 GB of RAM and 160 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14455.92 and static web serving throughput of 8.6 GB/sec, alongside weak single-core performance and low cryptographic execution speeds. This server offers a budget-friendly, resource-dense option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is best suited for static web serving, Redis database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose utility applications.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cax31 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 8 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for budget-conscious workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cax31 is an ARM64 virtual server with 8 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage for budget-friendly workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual server offering eight shared cores and local SSD storage for cost-effective multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core Ampere Altra ARM64 processor at 2.0 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 160 GB of local SSD storage
Shared CPU allocation model optimized for budget-friendly resource density
Delivers 8.6 GB/sec throughput for static web serving
Suited for Redis operations, software compilation, and web serving
